title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the translational velocity at impact, we need to consider both the horizontal and vertical components of the velocity.

title: Horizontal Velocity
text: The horizontal velocity of the propeller remains constant as it falls. What is the horizontal velocity of the aircraft?

title: Calculate Horizontal Velocity
text: The horizontal velocity of the propeller is the same as the aircraft's horizontal velocity, which is $$40.0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$.

title: Vertical Velocity
text: To find the vertical velocity at impact, use the formula for the final velocity of an object in free fall: v $$=$$ $$\sqrt{2g h}$$.

title: Calculate Vertical Velocity
text: Using the formula $$v=\sqrt{2g h}$$, where $$g=9.8$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$ and $$h=300$$ $$m$$, calculate the vertical velocity.

title: Combine Velocities
text: The translational velocity at impact is the vector sum of the horizontal and vertical velocities. Use the Pythagorean theorem to find the resultant velocity.

title: Calculate Resultant Velocity
text: Using the Pythagorean theorem, $$v=\sqrt{{v_{horizontal}}^2+{v_{vertical}}^2}$$, where $$v_{horizontal}=40.0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and $$v_{vertical}=76.68$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$.
